mechanism_narrative: Rising 3-month momentum in RSAFS signals resilient household demand and near-term earnings support for consumer-linked firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when retail sales growth is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:latest RSAFS snapshot was 497559 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce higher subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns, with Rank IC below 0.03 over two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising FEDFUNDS path tightens discount rates and financing conditions, reducing equity valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days after a policy-rate tightening threshold is crossed.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 0.25 percentage points over 3 months and is above 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:latest FEDFUNDS snapshot was 1.69 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered tightening observations are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time or if the average triggered return is not below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses below 4.0%, labor-market tightness signals late-cycle wage pressure and higher probability of restrictive monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest UNRATE snapshot was 3.9 as of one month before the decision date
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-below-4.0 triggered periods have 60-day S&P 500 returns equal to or above unconditional returns in walk-forward tests with p-value below 0.10 absent.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in INDPRO indicates improving real output and operating leverage, raising expected corporate revenues and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days when industrial activity trends upward.
evidence_refs: fact:latest INDPRO snapshot was 107.1665 as of two months before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO momentum fails to predict above-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than half of walk-forward validation windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while RSAFS decelerates, inflation pressure coincides with weakening real demand, compressing margins and raising policy-risk premia, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth rises above its 12-month average while RSAFS 3-month growth turns negative
evidence_refs: fact:latest CPIAUCSL snapshot was 250.013 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL-acceleration and RSAFS-deceleration trigger is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and deteriorating construction activity, leading investors to mark down cyclical earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average by more than 10%
evidence_refs: fact:latest HOUST snapshot was 1319.0 as of two months before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside-threshold events do not lead to lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered periods across two consecutive walk-forward samples.

mechanism_narrative: Strong PAYEMS growth combined with a falling UNRATE signals broad income creation without immediate recession pressure, supporting consumption and earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average gain exceeds 150 thousand and UNRATE is at or below its prior 3-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:latest PAYEMS snapshot was 148424.0 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-strength and UNRATE-decline interaction has a nonpositive mean 20-day S&P 500 forward return or a Rank IC below 0.03 in rolling validation.

mechanism_narrative: A short-horizon CPIAUCSL acceleration shock raises expected inflation compensation and the probability of hawkish repricing, reducing equity risk appetite and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 1-month growth crosses above its trailing 12-month average while FEDFUNDS is not declining
evidence_refs: fact:latest FEDFUNDS snapshot was 1.69 and CPIAUCSL snapshot was 250.013 before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL upside shocks with nondeclining FEDFUNDS are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time in walk-forward tests.
